item.title: How to stop Meta’s AI image generator from using your Instagram photos
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Meta's new AI image generator is using your public Instagram photos unless you opt out. Here's how to do that.

item.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

item.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

item.title: Block reaches $45M settlement with 46 states over Cash App fraud probe
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: State attorneys general said they found that Block misled users by falsely advertising that Cash App provided bank-like protections, including advanced fraud detection.

item.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

item.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

item.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: In an interesting twist that takes advantage of the company's core product, users can chat with these shows' characters, ask them questions, and even roleplay different storylines.

item.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Nilekani remains Fundamentum's anchor investor as the firm expands its leadership team and targets AI and fintech startups in India.

item.title: Truecaller clashes with India’s telecom regulator over anti-spam rules
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The caller ID company says users are increasingly ignoring and blocking calls from India's dedicated business number series.

item.title: Despite ‘misgivings,’ judge approves Elon Musk’s $1.5M SEC settlement
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The saga of Musk's tussle with the SEC over how he disclosed his growing stake in Twitter (now X) has come to an end.

item.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

item.title: Feds demand autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said emergency scenes are not "edge cases."

item.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Earlier this week, a picture seemed to show Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell covered in tubes in a hospital bed in a state of extreme distress. It turned out to be an AI-generated fake.

item.title: With EU backing, QuantumDiamonds aims to speed up chip manufacturing
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Like its U.S. counterpart, the European Chips Act aims to foster the semiconductor industry — in part thanks to state subsidies. One of the beneficiaries is QuantumDiamonds, a German startup that applies a novel approach to inspecting chips.

item.title: Autonomous drone delivery startup Manna plots major US expansion
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Manna is launching a U.S. operations and manufacturing facility in Tulsa, Oklahoma, that will eventually employ 1,000 people.

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: ‘Slow-cial’ app Roost forces you to slow down to the speed of a carrier pigeon
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: This developer didn't expect his side project to grow to 300,000 users, but people love Roost because it's an alternative to an always-on, fast-paced online culture.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.

item.title: Elon Musk says X will send DMs when posts you’ve engaged with are corrected
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X plans to send users direct messages when posts they’ve liked, replied to, or reposted receive Community Notes, an update aimed at addressing criticism that the platform’s crowdsourced fact-checking system often arrives too late to curb misinformation.
